tspan.data-narration: Common issues with stochastic models include non-stationarity, non-linearity, and autocorrelation.

text.tspan: Common issues with stochastic models include non-stationarity, non-linearity, and autocorrelation.

tspan.data-narration: Non-stationarity means the model is not capturing the changing characteristics of the time series over time.

text.tspan: Non-stationarity means the model is not capturing the changing characteristics of the time series over time.

tspan.data-narration: Non-linearity means the relationship between the independent and dependent variables is not linear.

text.tspan: Non-linearity means the relationship between the independent and dependent variables is not linear.

tspan.data-narration: Autocorrelation means the model is not able to capture the correlations between different time points in the data.

text.tspan: Autocorrelation means the model is not able to capture the correlations between different time points in the data.

tspan.data-narration: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits the training data too closely, resulting in poor performance on new, unseen data.

text.tspan: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits the training data too closely, resulting in poor performance on new, unseen data.

tspan.data-narration: This can lead to poor performance on new data, overestimation of the model's parameters, and increased risk of making incorrect predictions.

text.tspan: This can lead to poor performance on new data, overestimation of the model's parameters, and increased risk of making incorrect predictions.

tspan.data-narration: To avoid overfitting, it is essential to evaluate the model's performance on new, unseen data.

text.tspan: To avoid overfitting, it is essential to evaluate the model's performance on new, unseen data.

tspan.data-narration: Additionally, using techniques such as regularization and cross-validation can help to prevent overfitting.

text.tspan: Additionally, using techniques such as regularization and cross-validation can help to prevent overfitting.
